librarian_queries.query: Retrieve scoped lessons for task-assigner assignment planning on PR-468504.
librarian_queries.answer_summary: No assignment-stage lessons matched this task, so no prevention rules were applied.

librarian_queries.query: What scheduling-relevant knowledge should shape PR-468504 assignment planning?
librarian_queries.answer_summary: Use the dependency-safe four-wave sequence T1 -> T2 -> T3 -> T4, with shutdown wiring first, then core cancellation propagation, then secondary endpoint propagation, and finally follow-up documentation.

librarian_queries.query: What repo/file-level evidence in rls-orders-cnsmr-api matters for scheduling?
librarian_queries.answer_summary: Program.cs and GoogleCloudLoggingStartup.cs are the bootstrap shutdown surfaces, OrdersController and TestsController are the primary deep-propagation chains, and the secondary Accounts/Labels/Documents/Reports families follow afterward with partial shared coupling.

librarian_exploration_summaries.query: What existing repo touchpoints should shape graceful-shutdown planning in rls-orders-cnsmr-api?
librarian_exploration_summaries.summary_received: Prior exploration identified Program.cs and GoogleCloudLoggingStartup.cs as the bootstrap shutdown surfaces, with OrdersController and TestsController through service/helper/repository layers as the primary propagation chains.

librarian_exploration_summaries.query: What dependency order is safest for the implementation work?
librarian_exploration_summaries.summary_received: Existing repo knowledge supports the sequence T1 -> T2 -> T3 -> T4 so shutdown wiring is established before deeper cancellation propagation rolls out.

execution_schedule.batch_rationale: T1 is the bootstrap contract for graceful shutdown and Serilog flush behavior, so it must land before any downstream cancellation propagation work can be safely implemented.

uows.title: Implement host-lifecycle graceful shutdown and Serilog flush behavior
definition_of_done: Program.cs and related startup wiring explicitly implement graceful host shutdown behavior for Cloud Run on .NET 8/alpine.
The API has a defined mechanism to stop accepting new work once shutdown begins.
Serilog flush and close behavior is wired into shutdown without introducing unhandled exceptions.
Shutdown-focused automated coverage or equivalent verification is updated.
uows.rationale: Highest-risk foundational work: it defines the shutdown contract, stop-accepting-new-work behavior, and sink flush path that all later propagation work depends on.

execution_schedule.batch_rationale: T2 should start only after the shutdown contract exists, and it remains a single scheduled UoW because the core orders/tests paths may share abstractions that need coordinated cancellation-token changes.

uows.title: Propagate CancellationToken through core orders and tests request paths
definition_of_done: OrdersController and TestsController propagate CancellationToken into their service contracts.
Core orders/tests helpers, planning/execution helpers, and repository calls either accept the token or perform explicit cooperative cancellation checks.
Parallel or fanout work in the core paths honors cancellation cleanly.
Automated coverage is expanded for token propagation and cancellation behavior in the core request paths.
uows.rationale: This is the highest-value deep-propagation wave and de-risks the most important request paths before expanding the pattern to secondary endpoints.

execution_schedule.batch_rationale: T3 follows T2 so secondary endpoint chains can reuse the proven shutdown-aware pattern instead of discovering contract and abstraction changes independently.

uows.title: Extend the shutdown-aware cancellation pattern to remaining scoped endpoint chains
definition_of_done: Secondary controller and service chains in scope follow the same shutdown-aware cancellation pattern established in the core paths.
Shared abstractions that block token propagation are either updated in scope or clearly captured as implementation risks.
No scoped endpoint path is left bypassing the selected graceful-shutdown strategy.
Regression-focused automated coverage is updated for the additional changed paths.
uows.rationale: This batch broadens the established approach to the remaining in-scope families while keeping cross-cutting shared abstractions coordinated.

execution_schedule.batch_rationale: T4 closes the work only after implementation details and residual limitations are known from T1-T3.

uows.title: Document out-of-scope follow-up APIs and residual shutdown risks
definition_of_done: Implementation notes explicitly call out rls-orders-orch-api and rls-orders-data-api as out-of-scope follow-up work.
Residual risks and assumptions, including Cloud Run shutdown budget uncertainty and any token-propagation limits in shared abstractions, are documented.
The documentation matches the implemented repo scope and does not imply cross-repo changes were made here.
uows.rationale: This batch depends on the earlier implementation waves because it must reflect actual residual risks, assumptions, and out-of-scope follow-up APIs.

risk_ordered_items.risk_reason: Shutdown bootstrap behavior, stop-accepting-new-work semantics, and Serilog flush handling are foundational and still have an unresolved mechanism choice.
Core orders/tests propagation may require cross-cutting changes in shared service, helper, and repository abstractions to support CancellationToken cleanly.
Secondary endpoint chains include partially shared docgen-style infrastructure, so propagation depth may reveal coupled abstractions or cancellability gaps.
Documentation is low implementation risk but must capture any residual limitations from the earlier waves accurately.

batch_2_internal_workstreams.safety_rationale: These branches are the best internal split after T1 completes, but they remain inside one scheduled UoW because shared abstractions may still require coordinated edits.

batch_3_internal_workstreams.safety_rationale: Accounts and Reports look most separable after T2 proves the pattern, but Labels and Documents remain only partially parallelizable because they appear to converge on shared docgen/repository infrastructure.

metacognitive_context.decision_rationale: The simplest robust schedule is a four-batch sequence that mirrors the task-plan dependency chain and the repo evidence: first define the host shutdown contract, then propagate cancellation through the core request paths, then extend the same pattern to the remaining scoped endpoints, and finally document out-of-scope follow-up APIs and residual risks.

alternatives_discarded.approach: Parallelize T2 and T3 as separate implementation waves.
alternatives_discarded.reason_rejected: tasks.yaml declares T3 depends on T2, and repo evidence suggests secondary endpoint work should reuse the proven core-path pattern rather than establish it independently.

alternatives_discarded.approach: Split T2 and T3 into multiple scheduled UoWs for each controller family.
alternatives_discarded.reason_rejected: That would invent scope beyond the provided tasks and would overstate safe parallelism where shared abstractions may force coordinated edits.

alternatives_discarded.approach: Collapse T1-T3 into one implementation batch plus T4.
alternatives_discarded.reason_rejected: That would hide the highest-risk dependency boundaries between bootstrap shutdown wiring, core propagation, and secondary-path rollout.

knowledge_gaps: Exact Cloud Run termination grace period or shutdown budget for the deployed service.
Whether all shared HTTP/storage abstractions in scope already accept CancellationToken or need signature expansion.
Whether document/PDF-style dependencies in the secondary paths support cooperative cancellation.
Which concrete mechanism should stop new work once host shutdown begins.

tool_anomalies.anomaly: The librarian reported ov retrieval failures caused by 401 models-permission errors, so recovered flat-file knowledge and repo exploration were used as fallback evidence.
The repo-local validator still requires a top-level batches array, while downstream runtime/tests require execution_schedule, so this artifact carries both views for compatibility.
